issues.title: TAA-001 — Add AUDIT_ENABLED config and Tap-only startup validation
issues.description:
## Context

Add the explicit audit-mode gate described in `docs/tap-append-only-audit.md`. Audit history is Tap-only for the first implementation.

## Scope

- Add `AUDIT_ENABLED` env/config parsing.
- Keep default behavior unchanged when unset/false.
- If `AUDIT_ENABLED=true` and `TAP_ENABLED=false`, fail startup with a clear error explaining that audit storage currently requires Tap.
- Surface the flag in config docs/examples where config is defined.

## Acceptance criteria

- `AUDIT_ENABLED=false` does not alter ingestion wiring.
- `AUDIT_ENABLED=true` with Tap enabled is accepted.
- `AUDIT_ENABLED=true` without Tap fails startup with an actionable error.
- Unit/config tests cover the validation behavior if this repo already has config tests.

## Dependency graph

- Blocked by: none.
- Blocks: TAA-006, TAA-012.

issues.title: TAA-002 — Add audit event migrations for SQLite and Postgres
issues.description:
## Context

Create append-only audit storage for valid Tap record/identity deliveries without changing existing current-state `record` and `actor` table semantics.

## Scope

Add both up/down migrations under the embedded migration paths:

- `internal/database/migrations/sqlite/007_add_audit_events.up.sql`
- `internal/database/migrations/sqlite/007_add_audit_events.down.sql`
- `internal/database/migrations/postgres/007_add_audit_events.up.sql`
- `internal/database/migrations/postgres/007_add_audit_events.down.sql`

Tables:

- `raw_tap_events`: every successfully parsed record/identity Tap delivery, including duplicates.
- `record_events`: deduped append-only decoded record ledger.
- `identity_events`: append-only decoded identity ledger.

## Acceptance criteria

- SQLite and Postgres migrations define the fields, constraints, unique keys, foreign keys, and indexes from the plan.
- `record_events.event_key` and `identity_events.event_key` are unique.
- Down migrations remove only the audit tables/indexes added here.
- Existing current-state schema remains unchanged.

## Dependency graph

- Blocked by: none.
- Blocks: TAA-004, TAA-005, TAA-007, TAA-009.

issues.title: TAA-003 — Pass raw Tap payload and full event through HandleEvent
issues.description:
## Context

Audit ingest needs the raw websocket message bytes and the top-level Tap delivery id. The current Tap dispatch path only passes decoded record/identity payloads.

## Scope

- Change the Tap event handler interface to `HandleEvent(ctx context.Context, rawPayload []byte, event *tap.Event) error`.
- Make the consumer read raw websocket bytes, parse `tap.Event`, call the handler, then ack only after handler success.
- Preserve existing non-audit current-state behavior by adapting the existing handler implementation.
- Keep unsupported/malformed frame behavior intentionally simple for the first implementation.

## Acceptance criteria

- Handler receives the original raw payload bytes and full top-level `tap.Event`.
- Tap ack uses the top-level delivery `event.ID`.
- Existing Tap mode still works when audit is disabled.
- No Jetstream/backfill behavior is pulled into this change.

## Dependency graph

- Blocked by: none.
- Blocks: TAA-006, TAA-010.

issues.title: TAA-004 — Implement record audit ingest with dedupe and current-state transaction
TAA-005 — Implement identity audit ingest and current actor behavior
issues.description:
## Context

Identity Tap deliveries need their own append-only ledger while preserving existing current actor/identity behavior, including record purges.

## Scope

- Extend `AuditRepository.IngestTapEvent` for identity events.
- Insert one `raw_tap_events` row for each valid identity delivery.
- Compute best-effort event key: `identity:{tap_delivery_id}:{did}:{handle}:{is_active}:{status}`.
- Insert `identity_events` with conflict-do-nothing semantics.
- If inserted, apply existing Tap identity behavior for current `actor` state and record purging.
- Do not create synthetic per-record delete audit rows for identity purges.

## Acceptance criteria

- Identity event appends raw and identity audit rows.
- Current actor behavior remains equivalent to current Tap ingestion.
- Identity purges remove current records without adding synthetic `record_events` deletes.
- Duplicate identity event conflicts on `identity_events.event_key` and is treated as successful.

## Dependency graph

- Blocked by: TAA-002.
- Blocks: TAA-006, TAA-009.

issues.title: TAA-006 — Wire audit mode into Tap handler and ack-after-commit flow
issues.description:
## Context

When audit mode is enabled, the Tap index handler should delegate database writes to `AuditRepository` so audit inserts and current-state projection changes share one transaction.

## Scope

- Wire `AuditRepository` into service/container/repository setup.
- In Tap audit mode, route `IndexHandler.HandleEvent` to `AuditRepository.IngestTapEvent`.
- Avoid separate `RecordsRepository` / `ActorsRepository` writes in audit mode.
- Publish GraphQL subscriptions only after the ingest transaction succeeds.
- Treat duplicate decoded events as successful no-ops for current-state updates.
- Keep legacy activity logging best-effort after commit if it remains enabled.

## Acceptance criteria

- `AUDIT_ENABLED=false` preserves current Tap handler behavior.
- `AUDIT_ENABLED=true` writes audit rows and current-state updates in one transaction.
- Tap ack is sent only after repository commit succeeds.
- Duplicate decoded event still commits and acks.

## Dependency graph

- Blocked by: TAA-001, TAA-003, TAA-004, TAA-005.
- Blocks: TAA-010, TAA-012, TAA-013.

issues.title: TAA-007 — Add audit record event query repository with filters and cursors
issues.description:
## Context

GraphQL audit queries need a repository method that reads from `record_events` with stable opaque cursor pagination.

## Scope

- Add `FindRecordEvents(ctx, opts RecordEventFindOptions) (*RecordEventPage, error)` or equivalent.
- Support first-version filters: `id`, `uri`, `did`, `collection`, `rkey`, `action`, `live`, `rev`, `cid`, `receivedAt`.
- Support order by `record_events.id` ascending/descending initially, matching the plan examples.
- Encode opaque cursors around `record_events.id`.
- Keep query behavior dialect-compatible for SQLite and Postgres.

## Acceptance criteria

- Repository returns stable pages and `pageInfo` data needed by GraphQL.
- Filters compose safely with parameterized SQL.
- Cursor decoding errors are actionable.
- No existing current-state query path changes.

## Dependency graph

- Blocked by: TAA-002.
- Blocks: TAA-008.

issues.title: TAA-008 — Add GraphQL auditRecordEvents API
issues.description:
## Context

Expose append-only record history through a separate audit query alongside existing current-state queries. Avoid the name `RecordEvent` because subscriptions already use it.

## Scope

- Add GraphQL types for `AuditRecordEvent`, `AuditRecordAction`, filters, ordering, connection, edge, and page info integration.
- Add `Query.auditRecordEvents(first, after, where, orderBy)`.
- Wire `AuditRepository` through resolver repository context/service container.
- Map database action strings to enum values `CREATE`, `UPDATE`, `DELETE`.
- Return JSON record payloads.

## Acceptance criteria

- Existing GraphQL current-state APIs remain unchanged.
- `auditRecordEvents` supports plan examples for record URI trail, collection deletes, DID changes, and cursor continuation.
- Type naming avoids collision with subscription `RecordEvent`.
- Resolver errors explain invalid filters/cursors clearly.

## Dependency graph

- Blocked by: TAA-007.
- Blocks: TAA-011, TAA-012, TAA-013.

issues.title: TAA-009 — Add repository ingest tests for records, dedupe, identity, and purges
TAA-010 — Add Tap consumer and audit handler ack-flow tests
issues.description:
## Context

Tap is at-least-once. The consumer must acknowledge only after the audit/current-state transaction succeeds, while duplicates should still ack after a successful no-op transaction.

## Scope

- Test that raw payload and full event reach the handler.
- Test successful handler call emits `ack` with top-level Tap delivery id.
- Test handler error prevents ack.
- Test duplicate decoded event path returns success and acks.
- Test audit disabled path still uses existing current-state behavior.

## Acceptance criteria

- Ack behavior is covered without requiring a live Tap service.
- Tests clearly distinguish parse failure, handler failure, and duplicate no-op success.
- No Jetstream/backfill tests are added for this audit feature.

## Dependency graph

- Blocked by: TAA-003, TAA-006.
- Blocks: TAA-013.

issues.title: TAA-011 — Add GraphQL audit filter and cursor pagination tests
issues.description:
## Context

The user-facing audit API needs regression coverage for filtering and stable cursor behavior.

## Scope

- Test `auditRecordEvents` filters by `uri`, `did`, `collection`, and `action`.
- Test cursor pagination uses stable `record_events.id` cursors.
- Test ascending/descending ordering if both are supported in the resolver.
- Test invalid cursor/filter errors are actionable.

## Acceptance criteria

- GraphQL tests demonstrate the example query shapes from the plan.
- Pagination does not skip or duplicate rows across pages.
- Tests do not depend on wall-clock timing for row order.

## Dependency graph

- Blocked by: TAA-008.
- Blocks: TAA-013.

issues.title: TAA-012 — Document Tap audit mode, limitations, examples, and config
issues.description:
## Context

Operators need to understand what audit mode stores, what it does not reconstruct, how duplicates behave, and how to query the audit trail.

## Scope

- Document `AUDIT_ENABLED=true` with `TAP_ENABLED=true`.
- Explain that audit history starts when Tap audit ingestion starts and does not reconstruct older repo edits.
- Explain `live=true/false` semantics.
- Explain delete audit behavior vs current `record` removal.
- Explain duplicate Tap deliveries and `raw_tap_events`.
- Explain identity purges are represented by `identity_events`, not synthetic record deletes.
- Include the example GraphQL queries from the plan or equivalent.

## Acceptance criteria

- README or Tap deployment docs include config and limitations.
- Docs include at least one record audit trail query and one delete query.
- Docs match the implemented schema and names exactly.

## Dependency graph

- Blocked by: TAA-001, TAA-006, TAA-008.
- Blocks: TAA-013.

issues.title: TAA-013 — Final SQLite/Postgres verification and release-note fragment
issues.description:
## Context

Close the feature by running the repo-required verification for Go/database/API changes and adding a Changie fragment because this is externally meaningful for operators and API users.

## Scope

- Run SQLite verification first.
- Run Postgres verification if local Postgres is available.
- Run relevant GraphQL/API tests.
- Add a Changie fragment using the repository workflow.
- Fix dialect-specific issues found during verification.

## Acceptance criteria

- `go build -v ./...` passes.
- `make lint` passes or has documented unrelated blockers.
- `DATABASE_URL=sqlite::memory: go test -v -race ./...` passes.
- Postgres DB-backed tests pass when the local test database is available.
- `.changes/unreleased/*.yaml` includes the audit feature note.

## Dependency graph

- Blocked by: TAA-006, TAA-008, TAA-009, TAA-010, TAA-011, TAA-012.
- Blocks: none.
